title: Debug memsearch retrieval through Markdown file and index artifacts

items.text: memsearch retrieval-debug evidence: rerun memsearch search with --top-k, inspect the matching Markdown file, and rerun memsearch index after any file rewrite or delete.
memsearch debug boundary: the current adapter exposes CLI search output and canonical Markdown files, but it does not emit staged query-expansion, fusion, rerank, or candidate-drop trace bundles.

locator.quote: does not emit staged query-expansion, fusion, rerank, or candidate-drop trace bundles

items.text: Decoy: memsearch exposes the same staged retrieval trajectory and candidate-drop trace bundle as ELF.

answer.content: For memsearch retrieval debugging, rerun memsearch search with --top-k, inspect the matching Markdown file, and rerun memsearch index after file changes. The useful debug surface is source-file transparency plus CLI replay; staged expansion, fusion, rerank, and candidate-drop trace bundles are not emitted by the current adapter.

claims.text: Rerun memsearch search with --top-k and inspect the matching Markdown file.
The current adapter does not emit staged expansion, fusion, rerank, or candidate-drop trace bundles.

trace_explainability.failure_reason: memsearch exposes CLI replay and source Markdown inspection, not staged retrieval trace bundles.

stages.notes: CLI replay can reproduce the visible result set.
The Markdown file remains inspectable as canonical source.
Candidate-drop trace bundles are not encoded for memsearch.

timeline.summary: The retrieval-debug job points at CLI replay and source Markdown inspection.
The job keeps staged trace bundles as not encoded for memsearch.

prompt.content: How should I debug a wrong memsearch retrieval result, and what trace visibility is not available?

must_include.text: Rerun memsearch search with --top-k and inspect the matching Markdown file.
The current adapter does not emit staged expansion, fusion, rerank, or candidate-drop trace bundles.

must_not_include: memsearch exposes the same staged retrieval trajectory and candidate-drop trace bundle as ELF

required_evidence.quote: does not emit staged query-expansion, fusion, rerank, or candidate-drop trace bundles

debuggability.criteria: Names the available memsearch debug path.

evidence_grounding.criteria: Cites CLI/source debug and trace-boundary evidence.

workflow_helpfulness.criteria: Provides a concrete replay and reindex sequence.

trap_avoidance.criteria: Does not overclaim staged trace visibility.

hard_fail_rules: unsupported high-confidence claim about a required decision or fact
use of a negative trap marked failure_if_used = true

operator_debug.root_cause: memsearch debugging is available through CLI replay and canonical Markdown inspection, while staged candidate-drop trace bundles are not encoded.
operator_debug.dropped_candidate_visibility: not encoded; inspect CLI search output and Markdown source instead
cli_steps: edit or delete the canonical file if needed
rerun search from a fresh process

ux_gaps.description: No staged expansion/fusion/rerank/candidate-drop bundle is emitted by the current memsearch adapter.
